diagrams.title: Binary Tree for Traversal Examples
diagrams.description: A sample binary tree with nodes A(root), B(left of A), C(right of A), D(left of B), E(right of B), F(left of C) used to demonstrate all traversals
elements: B (left child of A), C (right child of A)
D (left of B), E (right of B), F (left of C)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding Preorder, Inorder, Postorder, and Level Order sequences with actual values

traversalResults.preorder: A B D E C F
traversalResults.inorder: D B E A F C
traversalResults.postorder: D E B F C A
traversalResults.levelOrder: A B C D E F

diagrams.title: Expression Tree for (a+b)*(c-d)
diagrams.description: Expression tree showing operators at internal nodes and operands at leaves
elements: a, b as left and right children of +
c, d as left and right children of -
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how traversals produce prefix and postfix notations from expression trees

diagrams.title: Stack and Queue Operations in Tree Traversal
diagrams.description: Visual showing stack for DFS (push nodes going down, pop coming back) and queue for BFS
elements: Stack: LIFO - like vertical stack of plates
Animation: Show enqueue/dequeue for level order
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how data structures enable different traversal strategies
